name: Real World :: release candidate assessment
objective: Ask Nova to assess whether the current release candidate is actually ready to ship and to separate blockers from deferrable work.
messages: Assess whether the current NYO System release candidate is really ready to ship. Separate true blockers from important but deferrable work.
What is the single shortest next push if we only close one thing before shipping?
success_signals: Nova distinguishes release truth from general project quality.
Nova identifies installed-copy truth and handoff truth as central release concerns when they are still open.
Nova gives a short priority-ordered answer instead of an unbounded brainstorm.
failure_signals: Nova treats every valid issue as equally urgent.
Nova ignores documented readiness or promotion evidence.
Nova gives vague motivational advice instead of operational triage.
evidence_to_review: grounding in docs and runtime state
notes: This task should expose whether Nova can reason like a release operator instead of only a feature assistant.
